title: 2001 Lexus SC430 vs. Mercedes-Benz CLK430 Comparison Test
summary: From the archive: Fancy convertibles of the Y2K era.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is revisiting a classic comparison test between two luxury convertibles from the early 2000s: the Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430. This archive piece evaluates how these premium vehicles stacked up against each other during the Y2K era.
whoInvolved: Lexus, Toyota's luxury division, and Mercedes-Benz, the German automotive luxury brand, both competed in the high-end convertible market. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, conducted the original testing and analysis of these vehicles.
whyMatters: These vehicles represent a defining moment in luxury convertible design when manufacturers were balancing performance, comfort, and cutting-edge technology for affluent buyers. Retrospective comparisons like this offer insight into how automotive engineering and consumer preferences have evolved over two decades.
moreContext: The early 2000s marked a golden age for luxury convertibles, as manufacturers invested heavily in this segment before market shifts toward SUVs and crossovers. The Lexus SC430, known for reliability and understated elegance, competed directly with Mercedes' CLK-Class convertible, which emphasized sporty handling and performance. Both vehicles featured retractable hard-top technology, a premium feature at the time. Archive content like this serves automotive enthusiasts and historians by documenting how different manufacturers approached luxury convertible design philosophy during this era.

title: The Bestselling Luxury Vehicles of 2026
summary: Which models from the likes of BMW, Mercedes, and Lexus are the biggest hitters on the sales charts? We've gathered the numbers and ranked the top 10 sellers.
whatHappening: Car and Driver has released its rankings of the top 10 best-selling luxury vehicles in 2026. The analysis reveals which models from premium automakers are dominating consumer preferences and sales charts.
whoInvolved: Major luxury automotive brands BMW, Mercedes-Benz, and Lexus are the primary players featured in this sales analysis. These three manufacturers represent some of the world's most established and competitive segments in the premium vehicle market.
whyMatters: Understanding which luxury models lead in sales provides insight into consumer spending patterns and preferences during an economic period. The rankings signal which automakers are successfully capturing high-margin vehicle sales and maintaining market dominance in the lucrative luxury segment.
moreContext: The luxury vehicle market has undergone significant shifts in recent years with the rise of luxury SUVs and crossovers overshadowing traditional sedans. Electric and hybrid powertrains have increasingly become standard offerings across luxury lineups as manufacturers respond to environmental concerns and changing buyer preferences. 2026 represents a critical year as the automotive industry navigates the transition toward electrification while maintaining brand prestige. Sales data from this period reflects consumer appetite for premium features, performance, and sustainability in high-end vehicles.

title: View Photos of the 2001 Lexus SC430 vs. Mercedes-Benz CLK430
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 2001 Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430 from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is publishing a visual comparison of two luxury convertibles from the early 2000s: the 2001 Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430. Readers can view both vehicles from multiple angles to compare their interior and exterior design.
whoInvolved: Lexus, Toyota's luxury division, and Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, both competed in the premium convertible segment during the early 2000s. Car and Driver is a leading automotive publication known for comprehensive vehicle comparisons and photography.
whyMatters: These comparisons help enthusiasts and potential buyers understand the design philosophies and practical differences between competing luxury vehicles. For classic car collectors and automotive historians, visual documentation of these models preserves important styling details from a significant era in convertible design.
moreContext: The 2001 model year represents the first generation SC430, which debuted as Lexus's answer to the Mercedes-Benz SL-Class and CLK-Class convertibles. The CLK430 was Mercedes' mid-range luxury convertible offering, positioned below the iconic SL-Class. Both vehicles featured retractable hardtops, a cutting-edge technology for convertibles at the time, and competed for affluent buyers seeking open-air luxury. The early 2000s marked a golden era for premium convertibles before market shifts toward SUVs and crossovers began reshaping luxury automakers' lineups.

title: This 2012 Cadillac CTS-V Wagon on Bring a Trailer Has a Little Extra
summary: A wider stance and more horsepower make this hot rod Caddy wagon not like the others.
whatHappening: A customized 2012 Cadillac CTS-V Wagon is being auctioned on Bring a Trailer with performance and styling upgrades that distinguish it from the standard model. The vehicle features a wider stance and increased horsepower compared to the factory version.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer, the online auction platform specializing in collectible and enthusiast vehicles, is hosting the sale. The car is a Cadillac CTS-V Wagon, General Motors' high-performance take on their mid-size sedan platform.
whyMatters: Customized performance vehicles command premium prices on specialty auction platforms, reflecting growing collector interest in modified American muscle cars and wagons. This sale demonstrates the market's appetite for unique automotive builds that combine practicality with serious performance upgrades.
moreContext: The Cadillac CTS-V Wagon was already a rare offering—GM produced it for only two generations (2009-2014) before discontinuing it, making it a niche collector vehicle. Wagon enthusiasts have increasingly turned to modified versions of performance models to combine cargo versatility with track-capable power. Bring a Trailer has become the premier marketplace for such builds, regularly attracting six-figure bids for distinctive custom vehicles. The CTS-V platform, powered by a 6.2L supercharged V8, provides an excellent foundation for modifications that can significantly exceed its 556 factory horsepower.

title: Bizzarrini Brings a 1960s Beauty to Life with Modern Engineering
summary: Only 10 of these gorgeous targa-topped throwbacks will be built.
whatHappening: Bizzarrini is reviving a classic 1960s sports car design with modern engineering and will produce only 10 examples of this targa-topped vehicle. The limited production run combines vintage aesthetics with contemporary performance and technology.
whoInvolved: Bizzarrini is an Italian automaker known for creating high-performance sports cars, particularly the iconic 5300 GT from the 1960s. The company has returned to manufacturing after decades away from production.
whyMatters: This project taps into the growing luxury market for low-volume, hand-crafted vehicles that blend retro design with modern engineering. The extremely limited production (10 units) ensures exclusivity and collector appeal while positioning Bizzarrini as a niche player in the boutique automaker space.
moreContext: The original Bizzarrini 5300 GT was a race-winning masterpiece from the 1960s that competed against Ferraris and other European supercars. The resurgence of retro-inspired sports cars has gained momentum in recent years, with brands like Nissan Z and Toyota GR86 drawing on heritage design language. Bizzarrini's return to production capitalizes on both nostalgia for classic Italian design and wealthy collectors' appetite for limited-edition vehicles with authentic provenance. The targa-top configuration offers an open-air driving experience while maintaining structural rigidity and modern safety standards.

title: Could the Currently Banned Polestar 3 Survive as a Volvo?
summary: Built in South Carolina, the Polestar 3 shares its key hardware and software with the Volvo EX90.
whatHappening: The Polestar 3, currently banned from the U.S. market, could potentially continue sales under the Volvo brand name. The vehicles share identical core hardware and software, suggesting a rebrand could bypass current restrictions.
whoInvolved: Polestar is Volvo's performance-focused EV subbrand, while Volvo manufactures the Polestar 3 at its South Carolina facility. Both companies are owned by Chinese conglomerate Geely-Volvo.
whyMatters: A successful rebrand could allow Volvo to maintain U.S. EV sales and production at its South Carolina plant while circumventing regulatory barriers. This approach would preserve jobs and revenue streams threatened by the Polestar 3's ban, while testing whether corporate restructuring can overcome trade or regulatory obstacles.
moreContext: The Polestar 3 faces U.S. market restrictions due to regulatory or trade-related issues stemming from its Chinese ownership structure. The vehicle shares technical platforms with the Volvo EX90, another premium EV from the same corporate family. Volvo has a long-established U.S. presence and manufacturing operations, potentially giving it different regulatory standing than the newer Polestar brand. This situation reflects broader tensions between Chinese automotive investment in the U.S. and government scrutiny of foreign ownership in the EV sector.

title: A Classic Hyundai Truck Stars in a Hugely Popular Retro-Style Game
summary: The lighthearted 'Dave the Diver' has sold in the millions, and now it's got an official Hyundai partnership.
whatHappening: Hyundai has partnered with the wildly successful retro-style game 'Dave the Diver' to feature a classic Hyundai truck in the game. The collaboration marks an official brand integration into the lighthearted indie title that has captivated millions of players.
whoInvolved: Hyundai, the South Korean automotive manufacturer, is partnering with the creators of 'Dave the Diver,' an indie game that became a cultural phenomenon. The game, known for its charming pixel art and fishing-based gameplay, has achieved multi-million unit sales across platforms.
whyMatters: This partnership signals how gaming has become a mainstream advertising channel that even traditional automakers are leveraging to reach younger audiences. For Hyundai, embedding its vehicles in a beloved indie game offers organic brand exposure without aggressive marketing, while the game gains official automotive partnerships that enhance its retro-futuristic world.
moreContext: 'Dave the Diver' exploded in popularity since its 2022 release, becoming one of the most successful indie games in recent years with millions of downloads on mobile and console platforms. The game's retro aesthetic and nostalgic charm have made it a cultural touchstone among Gen Z and millennial gamers. Hyundai's decision to partner with the game reflects broader automotive industry trends of brands seeking authenticity through gaming integrations rather than traditional advertising. Classic or vintage vehicle aesthetics align perfectly with the game's retro-style pixel art aesthetic, making the partnership feel natural rather than forced.

title: VW Could Cut Half Its Global Lineup to Prioritize Most Profitable Models
summary: The big changes likely won't have a huge effect on Volkswagen's U.S. lineup, but they could lead to fewer variants and trim levels.
whatHappening: Volkswagen is considering slashing its global vehicle lineup by up to 50% to focus resources on its most profitable models. The automaker is evaluating which vehicles and variants to keep as it restructures its portfolio.
whoInvolved: Volkswagen Group, the world's largest automaker by volume, is the company driving this strategic shift. The decision affects the German manufacturer's operations across multiple global markets and its various brands.
whyMatters: Cutting unprofitable models could significantly improve VW's margins and allow it to invest more heavily in electric vehicles and new technologies where competition is intensifying. While U.S. customers may see fewer trim and variant options rather than complete model eliminations, the move signals VW's aggressive pivot toward profitability in a rapidly shifting automotive market.
moreContext: Volkswagen has faced mounting pressure to reduce costs and accelerate its EV transition while competing against Tesla and Chinese automakers. The company previously announced significant restructuring plans including potential plant closures in Germany and workforce reductions. This portfolio consolidation aligns with industry-wide trends as traditional automakers struggle with the capital-intensive shift to electric vehicles. The strategy reflects VW's need to streamline operations and focus on segments where it can maintain competitive advantages and healthy profit margins.

title: 2027 Audi RS Q8
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Audi is unveiling the 2027 RS Q8, an updated performance SUV that combines luxury features with high-powered engineering. Car and Driver is providing a comprehensive review along with pricing and specification details for the refreshed model.
whoInvolved: Audi, the German luxury automaker owned by Volkswagen Group, is the manufacturer behind the RS Q8. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is covering the vehicle's debut with detailed analysis and specs.
whyMatters: The 2027 RS Q8 represents Audi's commitment to the performance luxury SUV segment, which continues to grow as consumers prioritize high-powered vehicles with premium features. This refresh signals how traditional automakers are evolving their lineups in response to changing market preferences and competitive pressures from newer EV-focused manufacturers.
moreContext: The RS Q8 is Audi's flagship performance variant of the Q8 luxury SUV, sitting at the top of the brand's lineup. The model has gained traction since its 2020 introduction as performance SUVs have become increasingly popular among affluent buyers seeking both speed and practicality. Audi's 2027 update cycle reflects the typical refresh pattern automakers use to maintain competitiveness, introduce new technology, and update styling. The luxury performance SUV segment remains highly competitive, with rivals including BMW's X6 M, Mercedes-AMG GLE 63, and Porsche Cayenne Turbo.

title: 2027 Land Rover Defender Updates Include Ditching the Turbo Four
summary: Land Rover's bestselling SUV also adds a stylish Vertex model and a cornucopia of new personalization options.
whatHappening: Land Rover is overhauling its bestselling Defender SUV for 2027, ditching the turbocharged four-cylinder engine in favor of new powertrain options. The refresh also introduces a stylish new Vertex model variant and expanded personalization choices for buyers.
whoInvolved: Land Rover, the British luxury SUV manufacturer owned by Jaguar Land Rover (part of Tata Motors), is making these changes to its flagship Defender model, which has become the company's volume seller since its 2020 relaunch.
whyMatters: The powertrain shift suggests Land Rover is responding to market demand for different engine options, possibly electrified alternatives or larger displacement engines that better suit the Defender's premium positioning. The expanded personalization and new Vertex trim indicate the automaker is working to increase model diversity and appeal to buyers seeking more distinctive styling in the competitive luxury SUV segment.
moreContext: The Defender was revived in 2020 after a 20-year hiatus, becoming Land Rover's sales leader and a critical success for the brand. The current generation uses a 2.0-liter turbocharged four-cylinder as its base engine. Adding the Vertex variant follows industry trends of creating sub-brands and special editions to justify premium pricing and differentiate models across trim levels. This 2027 update represents the first major refresh cycle for the modern Defender generation.

title: 2027 Toyota Highlander Production Delayed Due to 'Final Adjustments'
summary: The new EV SUV was supposed to go on sale by the end of the year, but now it may not arrive until 2027 after an eight-week delay.
whatHappening: Toyota is pushing back the launch of its new 2027 Highlander EV SUV by eight weeks due to final adjustments. The vehicle was originally scheduled to hit dealerships by year's end but will now arrive in 2027.
whoInvolved: Toyota, Japan's largest automaker and a global leader in hybrid and electric vehicle technology, is responsible for the delay. The company has been aggressively expanding its EV lineup to compete with Tesla and traditional automakers pivoting to electrification.
whyMatters: The delay could affect Toyota's EV sales targets and give competitors more runway to capture early adopters in the premium electric SUV segment. For consumers, the postponement means longer wait times for what's expected to be a flagship electric vehicle in Toyota's expanding lineup.
moreContext: The Highlander has long been one of Toyota's best-selling three-row SUVs, and the electric version represents a major shift in the brand's electrification strategy. This delay comes as automakers face supply chain pressures, battery sourcing challenges, and the need for extensive testing on new EV platforms. Toyota has committed to launching multiple electric vehicles through 2030 as part of its broader carbon neutrality goals. The eight-week adjustment period suggests potential issues with battery integration, software calibration, or safety systems that require additional refinement.

title: Mercedes-AMG CLA45 EV Revealed as 670-Horsepower Forbidden Fruit
summary: The luxury sport compact sedan ditched its turbocharged four-banger for a much more powerful trio of axial-flux electric motors.
whatHappening: Mercedes-AMG has unveiled an electric version of its CLA45 compact sport sedan, replacing the traditional turbocharged four-cylinder engine with three axial-flux electric motors that generate 670 horsepower. The new EV represents a dramatic power increase and a significant shift in the brand's approach to performance vehicles.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-AMG, the high-performance division of Mercedes-Benz, has long been known for engineering potent turbocharged engines in compact and mid-size vehicles. The company is one of the automotive industry's leading luxury performance brands competing directly with BMW M and Audi RS divisions.
whyMatters: The CLA45 EV signals that even traditional combustion-focused performance brands must embrace electrification to meet emissions regulations and consumer demands. This move could influence how luxury automakers balance performance credentials with environmental mandates, setting expectations for future AMG models and the broader premium automotive market.
moreContext: The original CLA45 AMG debuted in 2013 as a compact sedan with roughly 360 horsepower, establishing itself as an entry point to the AMG performance lineup. Mercedes has gradually expanded its electrified portfolio, but this marks one of the most powerful electric offerings from a traditional luxury performance marque. The shift to axial-flux motors—a newer, more compact motor technology—allows manufacturers to package multiple motors efficiently while delivering dramatic power outputs. This electric variant reflects broader industry trends where EV powertrains are becoming competitive or superior to traditional engines in raw performance metrics.

title: 2027 Maserati Grecale Folgore's Price Cut Is Much More Than a Fiat Topolino
summary: The electric version of the Grecale is heavily discounted, with Maserati slashing over $22,000 off last year's starting price.
whatHappening: Maserati is significantly cutting the price of the 2027 Grecale Folgore electric SUV, slashing over $22,000 from last year's starting price. This aggressive pricing move marks a major shift in the Italian luxury automaker's EV strategy.
whoInvolved: Maserati, the Italian luxury vehicle brand owned by Stellantis, is behind the price reduction. The company competes in the premium electric SUV segment against established players like Tesla, Porsche, and Mercedes-Benz.
whyMatters: The steep discount makes the Grecale Folgore significantly more accessible to premium car buyers, potentially boosting sales in a competitive EV market. This pricing strategy could force competitors to reevaluate their own electric vehicle pricing to remain competitive.
moreContext: The Grecale Folgore is Maserati's electric iteration of its popular Grecale SUV lineup, as the brand transitions toward electrification. The luxury automotive sector has seen increased price pressures as EV adoption accelerates and competition intensifies. Stellantis has been pushing aggressive electrification across its portfolio to meet emissions regulations and consumer demand. This price cut reflects broader industry trends where EV prices are falling as battery costs decrease and production scales up.

title: 2027 Telo MT1
summary: What We Know So Far
whatHappening: Car and Driver is reporting initial details about the upcoming 2027 Telo MT1, a new vehicle set to debut in the coming years. The publication is compiling what's known about this model so far as it approaches its release.
whoInvolved: Telo is the automaker behind the MT1 model. Car and Driver, a leading automotive journalism publication, is covering the vehicle's development and specifications.
whyMatters: The 2027 MT1 represents a new entry in its market segment that could influence buyer decisions and competitor strategies. Early information helps potential customers and industry watchers understand what's coming and how it might reshape the competitive landscape.
moreContext: The automotive industry continues to introduce new models across various segments to meet evolving consumer demands. Car and Driver's "What We Know So Far" approach reflects how manufacturers and media handle product launches, gradually revealing specifications and features ahead of official announcements. This incremental disclosure builds anticipation and allows for early market analysis. The 2027 timeline suggests this is still in development or pre-production phases, with final details potentially still subject to change.

title: Honda Recalls 325K Odyssey Minivans Due to Rearview Camera Issue
summary: Recall documents show that despite a previous recall, water can get into the rearview camera of some Odyssey models, potentially ruining the camera system.
whatHappening: Honda is recalling 325,000 Odyssey minivans because water is infiltrating the rearview camera system and causing it to fail. This is a repeat problem—the issue persists despite Honda's previous recall attempt to fix the same defect.
whoInvolved: Honda, the Japanese automotive manufacturer and one of the world's largest carmakers, is issuing the recall for its Odyssey minivan line. The company has faced ongoing quality control challenges with this particular component.
whyMatters: A non-functional rearview camera compromises driver visibility and safety when backing up, increasing accident risk—especially dangerous in a family vehicle like a minivan. This repeat failure undermines customer trust and suggests Honda's initial fix was inadequate, potentially exposing the company to liability and regulatory scrutiny.
moreContext: The rearview camera became a standard safety feature on new vehicles starting in 2018, mandated by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration. Honda had already attempted to address water ingress issues in the Odyssey's camera system through a prior recall, making this follow-up recall particularly problematic for the company's reputation. The persistence of the defect despite previous remedial action indicates a systemic engineering or manufacturing problem rather than an isolated issue. Water intrusion in vehicle electronics is a known durability challenge, especially in minivans that face frequent weather exposure and car wash environments.

title: 2028 Ford Ranchero Is a $30,000 Electric Truck Worth Waiting For
summary: While Ford hasn't yet confirmed the name—or many other details, for that matter—its upcoming EV aims to change the game.
whatHappening: Ford is developing an electric pickup truck expected to launch around 2028 with a starting price near $30,000. While the automaker hasn't officially confirmed the vehicle's name or full specifications, early indications suggest it could be a game-changing entry in the EV truck market.
whoInvolved: Ford Motor Company, one of America's largest automakers with a storied history in truck manufacturing, is spearheading this project. The company has been aggressively expanding its electric vehicle lineup to compete with Tesla and other EV makers.
whyMatters: A $30,000 electric truck could democratize EV ownership by making electric vehicles accessible to mainstream truck buyers who've traditionally been priced out of the EV market. This move positions Ford to capture a significant share of the growing electric truck segment and maintain its dominance in the truck category it has long dominated with gas-powered vehicles.
moreContext: Ford has invested heavily in EV development over the past five years, launching vehicles like the Mustang Mach-E and F-150 Lightning to compete in the electric market. The truck segment remains critical to Ford's profitability and brand identity, making an affordable EV option strategically important. Competitors like Tesla (Cybertruck) and Chevrolet (Silverado EV) are already moving into this space, intensifying pressure on Ford. The $30,000 price point would undercut most current electric truck offerings, potentially reshaping market expectations around EV pricing.

title: 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S-Class
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is unveiling the 2027 Maybach S-Class, the automaker's ultra-luxury flagship sedan that combines cutting-edge technology with handcrafted elegance. The vehicle represents the latest iteration of Mercedes' most exclusive and expensive passenger car offering.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automotive manufacturer, is behind this release. The Maybach brand, originally a separate German automaker acquired by Daimler (Mercedes' parent company), has been revived as Mercedes' ultra-premium sub-brand since 2015.
whyMatters: The 2027 Maybach S-Class signals how legacy luxury automakers are competing in an increasingly electric and technology-driven market while maintaining exclusivity and heritage. This refresh matters to high-net-worth consumers and investors watching whether traditional luxury can adapt to changing buyer preferences around sustainability and autonomous features.
moreContext: The Maybach brand was dormant for decades before Mercedes relaunched it in 2015 to compete with Rolls-Royce and Bentley in the ultra-luxury segment. The current generation Maybach S-Class debuted in 2021 as an extended-wheelbase variant of the S-Class with bespoke interiors and personalization options. Mercedes has been incrementally updating this platform with new powertrains, infotainment systems, and design elements. The 2027 refresh comes as luxury automakers face pressure to electrify their lineups while maintaining the hand-assembled craftsmanship and exclusivity that justify $150,000+ price tags.

title: Audi Sport Quattro Makes 500+ Horsepower Return in Restomod Form
summary: Sure, it costs about as much as a nice house, but it looks properly mean and claims a better power-to-weight ratio than the Ferrari SF90.
whatHappening: Audi Sport is reviving the legendary Quattro with a high-performance restomod that produces over 500 horsepower. The modernized classic delivers a power-to-weight ratio that surpasses Ferrari's SF90 supercar.
whoInvolved: Audi Sport, the performance division of German automaker Audi, is behind this project. The company has a storied history of rally racing heritage and performance engineering, dating back to the original Quattro's dominance in the 1980s.
whyMatters: This restomod signals automakers' growing interest in reimagining classic models with modern performance technology as combustion engines face regulatory pressure. It appeals to enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices for exclusive, heritage-based vehicles with contemporary capabilities.
moreContext: The original Audi Sport Quattro debuted in 1980 as a rally legend, revolutionizing motorsport with its all-wheel-drive system. This restomod continues Audi's tradition of blending historic design with cutting-edge engineering. The project reflects broader industry trends where manufacturers create limited-edition restomods to celebrate brand heritage while commanding luxury price points comparable to supercars. The vehicle's positioning against the Ferrari SF90—a $500,000+ plug-in hybrid—underscores how restomod economics compete directly with new exotic cars.

title: Trump's Latest Pardons Include Diesel Tuners Who Violated Clean Air Act
summary: Nine people convicted of bypassing emissions controls or selling equipment to do so have had their convictions overturned.
whatHappening: Trump has pardoned nine people convicted of selling or installing diesel engine tuning devices that bypass emissions controls. The convictions have been overturned, freeing individuals prosecuted under clean air regulations.
whoInvolved: The pardoned individuals were convicted under federal environmental laws for modifying diesel engines, primarily pickup trucks, to remove pollution-control systems. Trump's pardon authority allows him to overturn convictions without requiring congressional approval.
whyMatters: The pardons signal a rollback of EPA enforcement priorities and weaken deterrents against emissions violations in the diesel aftermarket industry. This could embolden similar modifications and undermine decades of clean air standards while potentially facing legal challenges from environmental groups.
moreContext: Diesel tuning has been a persistent enforcement issue, with the EPA and DOJ prosecuting sellers of defeat devices—equipment designed to circumvent emissions standards—since at least the early 2000s. The practice increased visibility after the 2015 Volkswagen dieselgate scandal exposed how manufacturers used software to cheat emissions tests. These tuning modifications increase nitrogen oxide emissions and particulate matter, contributing to respiratory illness and smog. Trump's pardon aligns with his administration's pattern of reducing environmental regulations and reflecting skepticism toward climate and air quality enforcement.

title: 2026 Fiat Topolino
summary: What We Know So Far
whatHappening: Fiat is reviving the iconic Topolino nameplate for 2026 with a new electric vehicle. The compact car will target budget-conscious European buyers seeking affordable EV options.
whoInvolved: Fiat, the Italian automaker owned by Stellantis, is leading the revival. The company has a history of producing affordable, practical vehicles dating back to the original Topolino (1936-1955), which became a symbol of accessible transportation in post-war Europe.
whyMatters: The new Topolino represents Fiat's strategy to democratize electric vehicles by offering an affordable entry point into EV ownership for mass-market consumers. This move is critical as European automakers face pressure to transition away from combustion engines while maintaining accessibility across income levels.
moreContext: The original Topolino was one of Italy's most beloved cars and helped motorize the nation in the mid-20th century. Stellantis has been aggressive in launching affordable EVs across its portfolio to compete with Chinese manufacturers and Tesla in the budget segment. Fiat previously launched the 500 electric as a premium-priced option, but the Topolino revival signals the brand is doubling down on its heritage of practical, cost-effective vehicles for everyday drivers.

title: 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 Drive: More, More, More
summary: How do you like it? If you want top-tier luxury with dashes of Mercedes flavoring, the V-8 Maybach delivers it all in ample doses.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Maybach has unveiled the 2027 S580, a ultra-luxury sedan packed with premium features and a powerful V-8 engine. The vehicle represents the automaker's latest push into the high-end luxury segment with abundant comfort and performance.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind the Maybach line, which targets ultra-wealthy consumers seeking the pinnacle of automotive luxury. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, tested and reviewed the vehicle.
whyMatters: The 2027 S580 signals Mercedes' continued investment in the ultra-luxury market despite economic uncertainty and shifting consumer preferences toward electric vehicles. Success in this segment demonstrates demand remains strong among affluent buyers willing to pay premium prices for exclusivity and traditional luxury experiences.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate, originally a luxury brand from the 1920s-30s, was revived by Mercedes in 2002 as an ultra-premium sub-brand positioned above the S-Class. The V-8 engine choice reflects Mercedes' strategy to maintain gas powertrains in its highest-tier offerings, even as the broader automotive industry transitions to electrification. Maybach vehicles typically start at $180,000+ and cater to a niche market of executives, celebrities, and international elites seeking bespoke luxury experiences.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580
summary: See the exterior of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz has revealed exterior photos of the upcoming 2027 Maybach S580, showcasing the luxury sedan from multiple angles. Car and Driver is providing a comprehensive visual walkthrough of the vehicle's design and styling details.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind the 2027 Maybach S580. The Maybach sub-brand represents Mercedes' ultra-premium vehicle line, competing against Rolls-Royce and Bentley in the pinnacle luxury segment.
whyMatters: The 2027 Maybach S580 reveals Mercedes' design direction for its most exclusive sedan offering and signals the company's commitment to the ultra-luxury market. This preview helps potential buyers and industry observers understand what the next generation of Mercedes' flagship luxury vehicle will deliver in terms of styling and presence.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate, revived by Mercedes in 2021, has become increasingly important to the brand's luxury portfolio alongside its S-Class flagship. The S580 designation reflects the powertrain upgrade from the previous generation, positioning it as the range-topper. Luxury automakers regularly use exterior reveals and design previews to build anticipation ahead of official launches. The 2027 model year redesign represents a significant refresh cycle for this segment, where styling, engineering, and technology updates are closely watched by affluent consumers and automotive enthusiasts.

title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580
summary: See the interior of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is unveiling interior photos of its 2027 Maybach S580, offering a first look at the luxury sedan's cabin design and features from multiple angles. The imagery provides consumers with a comprehensive view of what to expect from the brand's ultra-premium flagship model.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is showcasing its Maybach line, which represents the pinnacle of the company's luxury vehicle offerings. The S580 is the newest iteration of this exclusive sedan segment.
whyMatters: These interior reveals signal Mercedes-Benz's commitment to competing in the ultra-luxury market against rivals like Rolls-Royce and Bentley. The 2027 model year updates help potential buyers and enthusiasts understand the latest innovations in high-end automotive interiors, influencing purchasing decisions in a segment where design and craftsmanship are paramount selling points.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate represents Mercedes' most exclusive division, historically catering to ultra-wealthy consumers and executives. The S580 designation continues Mercedes' naming convention for its flagship sedan line. Luxury automakers typically release interior photography ahead of formal reveals to generate anticipation and media coverage among affluent demographics. The 2027 model year suggests this represents a mid-cycle refresh or generational update, a common strategy for maintaining competitive positioning in the luxury segment where subtle design evolution commands significant attention.

title: Cadillac Vistiqs Recalled for Possibly Dangerous Power-Folding Third Row
summary: Following Hyundai's second- and third-row recall earlier this year, GM tested the fold function on the Vistiq and found a similar issue.
whatHappening: General Motors is recalling Cadillac Vistiqs due to a potentially dangerous malfunction in the power-folding third-row seat mechanism. GM discovered the issue during testing and found it mirrors a similar problem that prompted Hyundai to recall vehicles earlier this year.
whoInvolved: General Motors and its luxury brand Cadillac are issuing the recall for the Vistiq, their three-row electric crossover. Hyundai previously identified comparable power-folding seat defects in second- and third-row configurations of its own vehicles.
whyMatters: The faulty power-folding mechanism poses a safety risk to passengers, potentially causing unexpected seat collapse or entrapment. This recall adds to growing concerns about power seat reliability across the auto industry and could impact consumer confidence in premium electric vehicle features.
moreContext: Power-folding seats have become popular convenience features in modern SUVs and crossovers, particularly in premium segments where the Vistiq competes. Hyundai's recall earlier this year flagged similar issues with automated third-row folding systems, prompting other manufacturers like GM to audit their own designs. The Vistiq, launched as Cadillac's answer to three-row electric crossovers, relies heavily on electronic features and automation as selling points. This recall highlights the emerging quality challenges automakers face as they rapidly introduce complex power mechanisms in new electric vehicle platforms.

title: The Only Cizeta Moroder V16T Supercar Ever Made Can Now Be Yours
summary: This crazy 16-cylinder prototype spawned a few production cars, but here's the only one to have music legend Giorgio Moroder's name on the back.
whatHappening: A rare Cizeta Moroder V16T supercar—the only one ever made with music legend Giorgio Moroder's name on it—is being offered for sale. This exotic prototype features a massive 16-cylinder engine and represents a unique piece of automotive and music history.
whoInvolved: Giorgio Moroder, the legendary music producer and composer known for his work on Flashdance and Scarface soundtracks, lent his name to this supercar collaboration. Cizeta, an Italian supercar manufacturer, created the vehicle as a limited-production hypercar project.
whyMatters: This car represents a rare intersection of music, design, and automotive engineering that appeals to collectors of both supercar memorabilia and music history. Its sale could set a precedent for celebrity-branded exotic vehicles and highlights the continued demand for one-off prototype supercars in the luxury market.
moreContext: The Cizeta Moroder V16T was conceived in the 1980s as an ambitious supercar project that combined Italian craftsmanship with cutting-edge engineering. While the prototype spawned a limited number of production vehicles, this particular example is distinguished by Moroder's personal involvement and branding. The 16-cylinder engine was a bold engineering choice that set it apart from competitors of the era. This sale represents a rare opportunity to own a piece of automotive history that bridges the worlds of high-performance cars and music culture.

title: Sumo-Sized: 1997 SUVs Compared
summary: Once again, we're faced with the age-old question: Is bigger necessarily better?
whatHappening: Car and Driver is comparing 1997 SUV models to evaluate whether larger vehicles deliver better performance and value. The publication revisits the fundamental question of whether size translates to superiority in the SUV market.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is conducting the analysis. The piece examines various 1997 SUV manufacturers competing in the growing segment during the mid-1990s boom.
whyMatters: This comparison helps consumers navigate a rapidly expanding SUV market by clarifying which vehicles justify their size and premium pricing. The findings influence purchasing decisions during a period when SUVs are becoming mainstream alternatives to sedans and station wagons.
moreContext: The 1990s saw explosive growth in the SUV category as consumers prioritized perceived safety, cargo space, and commanding driving positions over fuel efficiency. Major manufacturers including Ford, Chevrolet, Dodge, and Toyota were aggressively expanding their SUV lineups to capture market share. This era marked the transition of SUVs from utilitarian work vehicles to family transportation, fundamentally reshaping the automotive industry. Car and Driver's comparative testing methodology helps establish benchmarks for evaluating vehicles across reliability, performance, and practicality metrics.

title: Elana Scherr: Restoring a 1970 Dodge Challenger, Again
summary: Second chances aren’t just for rom-coms. Sometimes project cars give you another shot at love—and paint.
whatHappening: Elana Scherr is undertaking a restoration project on a 1970 Dodge Challenger for the second time. The automotive enthusiast is getting another opportunity to bring the classic muscle car back to life, this time with a fresh approach to the work.
whoInvolved: Elana Scherr is a automotive journalist and TV personality known for her work covering cars and restoration projects. She's recognized in the automotive media space for her expertise and passion for classic vehicles.
whyMatters: The story highlights how passion projects can offer redemption and second chances, resonating with enthusiasts who've abandoned or restarted restoration work. It underscores the enduring appeal of 1970s American muscle cars and the personal connections owners develop with their vehicles.
moreContext: The 1970 Dodge Challenger represents the golden age of American muscle cars, a period when Detroit manufacturers competed fiercely on performance and style. Scherr's decision to restore the same model twice suggests either unfinished business from a previous attempt or a renewed commitment to perfecting the project. Classic car restoration has become increasingly popular among automotive enthusiasts and media personalities as a way to preserve automotive history. The Challenger specifically has experienced a resurgence in cultural relevance, appearing prominently in films and becoming a collector's favorite among muscle car aficionados.

title: 2000 Mitsubishi Pajero Mini on Bring a Trailer Is Perfect for a Peanuts Fan
summary: And we thought Snoopy only drove a Sopwith Camel.
whatHappening: A 2000 Mitsubishi Pajero Mini is listed for sale on Bring a Trailer, a popular online auction platform for classic and specialty vehicles. The listing has caught attention from Peanuts comic strip fans due to the vehicle's quirky charm and nostalgic appeal.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer is an online marketplace specializing in collector cars, classic vehicles, and automotive rarities. The Mitsubishi Pajero Mini is a compact SUV produced by Japanese automaker Mitsubishi, popular in Japan and select international markets.
whyMatters: This listing appeals to a niche but passionate audience of Peanuts enthusiasts and Japanese vehicle collectors, potentially driving up bidding interest beyond typical compact SUV buyers. The intersection of pop culture fandom and automotive collecting demonstrates how nostalgic appeal can drive collector car market demand.
moreContext: The Peanuts reference stems from the famous comic strip character Snoopy, whose fictional adventures often featured him imagining himself as a World War I flying ace piloting a Sopwith Camel. The Pajero Mini, produced from 1994 to 2012, became a cult favorite among compact SUV enthusiasts, especially in Japan where it remained popular for decades. Bring a Trailer has become known for highlighting quirky and culturally significant vehicles that attract bidders beyond traditional car collectors. The platform's auction format often results in strong prices for vehicles with compelling backstories or niche appeal.

title: The Toyota 4Runner's Defining Feature Isn't Four-Wheel Drive
summary: The 4Runner's lore is built on off-road adventure, sure. But it's also about that power window in the liftgate.
whatHappening: Car and Driver argues that the Toyota 4Runner's cultural identity extends far beyond its legendary off-road capabilities. The vehicle's defining characteristic is actually the power window in its liftgate—a seemingly minor feature that has become iconic to the SUV's legacy.
whoInvolved: Toyota manufactures the 4Runner, one of the longest-running SUV nameplates in automotive history. Car and Driver is a leading automotive publication that analyzes vehicles through both performance and cultural lenses.
whyMatters: This perspective reframes how consumers and enthusiasts think about vehicle identity and brand heritage. It highlights how seemingly small design details can become more memorable and meaningful than headline specifications, influencing purchasing decisions and brand loyalty.
moreContext: The 4Runner has been in production since 1984 and built a reputation as a rugged, adventure-capable SUV that could handle extreme terrain and conditions. Over decades, the power liftgate window became a practical feature that owners valued for convenience during off-road trips and daily use. The vehicle's cultural cachet derives not just from capability specs but from the accumulated small details and lived experiences of its owners, making it emblematic of how automotive legacy is constructed through both engineering and mythology.

title: Ford's $30,000 Electric Truck Comes into Focus in New Spy Photos
summary: We're still wondering what the future pickup-like EV will be called, but the name Ranchero feels like a real possibility.
whatHappening: Ford's upcoming affordable electric truck is taking shape, with new spy photos revealing design details of the highly anticipated $30,000 EV. The automaker hasn't officially confirmed the vehicle's name, though internal speculation suggests it could be called the Ranchero, reviving a classic nameplate.
whoInvolved: Ford is developing this electric pickup as part of its broader EV strategy to compete in the mass-market segment. The company has been aggressively investing in electric vehicles to challenge Tesla's dominance and meet changing consumer demand.
whyMatters: A $30,000 electric truck could democratize EV ownership by making electric pickups accessible to mainstream buyers, a critical market segment in the U.S. This move positions Ford to capture price-conscious consumers who've been priced out of premium EV offerings, potentially reshaping the affordable truck market.
moreContext: Ford has committed billions to EV development, already launching the Mustang Mach-E and F-150 Lightning. The auto industry is racing to offer affordable electric vehicles as battery costs decline and competition intensifies. The Ranchero name—originally used for a car-truck hybrid produced from 1959 to 1979—would signal Ford's intention to create a unique vehicle category rather than a direct F-150 rival. Pricing this model at $30,000 would undercut most current EV competitors and address the industry's push toward making electrification economically viable for average buyers.
